members.name: BresenhamLine
members.signature: public Vector2i[] BresenhamLine(Vector2i from, Vector2i to)
members.summary: Returns the [url=https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bresenham%27s_line_algorithm]Bresenham line[/url] between the [param from] and [param to] points. A Bresenham line is a series of pixels that draws a line and is always 1-pixel thick on every row and column of the drawing (never more, never less). Example code to draw a line between two [Marker2D] nodes using a series of [method CanvasItem.draw_rect] calls: [codeblock] func _draw(): for pixel in Geometry2D.bresenham_line($MarkerA.position, $MarkerB.position): draw_rect(Rect2(pixel, Vector2.ONE), Color.WHITE) [/codeblock]

members.name: ClipPolygons
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] ClipPolygons(PackedVector2Array polygonA, PackedVector2Array polygonB)
members.summary: Clips [param polygon_a] against [param polygon_b] and returns an array of clipped polygons. This performs [constant OPERATION_DIFFERENCE] between polygons. Returns an empty array if [param polygon_b] completely overlaps [param polygon_a]. If [param polygon_b] is enclosed by [param polygon_a], returns an outer polygon (boundary) and inner polygon (hole) which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise].

members.name: ClipPolylineWithPolygon
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] ClipPolylineWithPolygon(PackedVector2Array polyline, PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Clips [param polyline] against [param polygon] and returns an array of clipped polylines. This performs [constant OPERATION_DIFFERENCE] between the polyline and the polygon. This operation can be thought of as cutting a line with a closed shape.

members.name: ConvexHull
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array ConvexHull(PackedVector2Array points)
members.summary: Given an array of [Vector2]s, returns the convex hull as a list of points in counterclockwise order. The last point is the same as the first one.

members.name: DecomposePolygonInConvex
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] DecomposePolygonInConvex(PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Decomposes the [param polygon] into multiple convex hulls and returns an array of [PackedVector2Array].

members.name: ExcludePolygons
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] ExcludePolygons(PackedVector2Array polygonA, PackedVector2Array polygonB)
members.summary: Mutually excludes common area defined by intersection of [param polygon_a] and [param polygon_b] (see [method intersect_polygons]) and returns an array of excluded polygons. This performs [constant OPERATION_XOR] between polygons. In other words, returns all but common area between polygons. The operation may result in an outer polygon (boundary) and inner polygon (hole) produced which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise].

members.name: GetClosestPointToSegment
members.signature: public Vector2 GetClosestPointToSegment(Vector2 point, Vector2 s1, Vector2 s2)
members.summary: Returns the 2D point on the 2D segment ([param s1], [param s2]) that is closest to [param point]. The returned point will always be inside the specified segment.

members.name: GetClosestPointToSegmentUncapped
members.signature: public Vector2 GetClosestPointToSegmentUncapped(Vector2 point, Vector2 s1, Vector2 s2)
members.summary: Returns the 2D point on the 2D line defined by ([param s1], [param s2]) that is closest to [param point]. The returned point can be inside the segment ([param s1], [param s2]) or outside of it, i.e. somewhere on the line extending from the segment.

members.name: GetClosestPointsBetweenSegments
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array GetClosestPointsBetweenSegments(Vector2 p1, Vector2 q1, Vector2 p2, Vector2 q2)
members.summary: Given the two 2D segments ([param p1], [param q1]) and ([param p2], [param q2]), finds those two points on the two segments that are closest to each other. Returns a [PackedVector2Array] that contains this point on ([param p1], [param q1]) as well the accompanying point on ([param p2], [param q2]).

members.name: IntersectPolygons
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] IntersectPolygons(PackedVector2Array polygonA, PackedVector2Array polygonB)
members.summary: Intersects [param polygon_a] with [param polygon_b] and returns an array of intersected polygons. This performs [constant OPERATION_INTERSECTION] between polygons. In other words, returns common area shared by polygons. Returns an empty array if no intersection occurs. The operation may result in an outer polygon (boundary) and inner polygon (hole) produced which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise].

members.name: IntersectPolylineWithPolygon
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] IntersectPolylineWithPolygon(PackedVector2Array polyline, PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Intersects [param polyline] with [param polygon] and returns an array of intersected polylines. This performs [constant OPERATION_INTERSECTION] between the polyline and the polygon. This operation can be thought of as chopping a line with a closed shape.

members.name: IsPointInCircle
members.signature: public bool IsPointInCircle(Vector2 point, Vector2 circlePosition, float circleRadius)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param point] is inside the circle or if it's located exactly [i]on[/i] the circle's boundary, otherwise returns [code]false[/code].

members.name: IsPointInPolygon
members.signature: public bool IsPointInPolygon(Vector2 point, PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param point] is inside [param polygon] or if it's located exactly [i]on[/i] polygon's boundary, otherwise returns [code]false[/code].

members.name: IsPolygonClockwise
members.signature: public bool IsPolygonClockwise(PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Returns [code]true[/code] if [param polygon]'s vertices are ordered in clockwise order, otherwise returns [code]false[/code]. [b]Note:[/b] Assumes a Cartesian coordinate system where [code]+x[/code] is right and [code]+y[/code] is up. If using screen coordinates ([code]+y[/code] is down), the result will need to be flipped (i.e. a [code]true[/code] result will indicate counter-clockwise).

members.name: LineIntersectsLine
members.signature: public Variant LineIntersectsLine(Vector2 fromA, Vector2 dirA, Vector2 fromB, Vector2 dirB)
members.summary: Returns the point of intersection between the two lines ([param from_a], [param dir_a]) and ([param from_b], [param dir_b]). Returns a [Vector2], or [code]null[/code] if the lines are parallel. [code]from[/code] and [code]dir[/code] are [i]not[/i] endpoints of a line segment or ray but the slope ([code]dir[/code]) and a known point ([code]from[/code]) on that line. To get the intersection between two line segments, use [method segment_intersects_segment]. [codeblocks] [gdscript] var from_a = Vector2.ZERO var dir_a = Vector2.RIGHT var from_b = Vector2.DOWN # Returns Vector2(1, 0) Geometry2D.line_intersects_line(from_a, dir_a, from_b, Vector2(1, -1)) # Returns Vector2(-1, 0) Geometry2D.line_intersects_line(from_a, dir_a, from_b, Vector2(-1, -1)) # Returns null Geometry2D.line_intersects_line(from_a, dir_a, from_b, Vector2.RIGHT) [/gdscript] [csharp] var fromA = Vector2.Zero; var dirA = Vector2.Right; var fromB = Vector2.Down; // Returns new Vector2(1, 0) Geometry2D.LineIntersectsLine(fromA, dirA, fromB, new Vector2(1, -1)); // Returns new Vector2(-1, 0) Geometry2D.LineIntersectsLine(fromA, dirA, fromB, new Vector2(-1, -1)); // Returns null Geometry2D.LineIntersectsLine(fromA, dirA, fromB, Vector2.Right); [/csharp] [/codeblocks]

members.name: MakeAtlas
members.signature: public Dictionary MakeAtlas(PackedVector2Array sizes)
members.summary: Given an array of [Vector2]s representing tiles, builds an atlas. The returned dictionary has two keys: [code]points[/code] is a [PackedVector2Array] that specifies the positions of each tile, [code]size[/code] contains the overall size of the whole atlas as [Vector2i].

members.name: MergePolygons
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] MergePolygons(PackedVector2Array polygonA, PackedVector2Array polygonB)
members.summary: Merges (combines) [param polygon_a] and [param polygon_b] and returns an array of merged polygons. This performs [constant OPERATION_UNION] between polygons. The operation may result in an outer polygon (boundary) and multiple inner polygons (holes) produced which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise].

members.name: OffsetPolygon
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] OffsetPolygon(PackedVector2Array polygon, float delta, int joinType)
members.summary: Inflates or deflates [param polygon] by [param delta] units (pixels). If [param delta] is positive, makes the polygon grow outward. If [param delta] is negative, shrinks the polygon inward. Returns an array of polygons because inflating/deflating may result in multiple discrete polygons. Returns an empty array if [param delta] is negative and the absolute value of it approximately exceeds the minimum bounding rectangle dimensions of the polygon. Each polygon's vertices will be rounded as determined by [param join_type]. The operation may result in an outer polygon (boundary) and inner polygon (hole) produced which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise]. [b]Note:[/b] To translate the polygon's vertices specifically, multiply them to a [Transform2D]: [codeblocks] [gdscript] var polygon = PackedVector2Array([Vector2(0, 0), Vector2(100, 0), Vector2(100, 100), Vector2(0, 100)]) var offset = Vector2(50, 50) polygon = Transform2D(0, offset) * polygon print(polygon) # Prints [(50.0, 50.0), (150.0, 50.0), (150.0, 150.0), (50.0, 150.0)] [/gdscript] [csharp] Vector2[] polygon = [new Vector2(0, 0), new Vector2(100, 0), new Vector2(100, 100), new Vector2(0, 100)]; var offset = new Vector2(50, 50); polygon = new Transform2D(0, offset) * polygon; GD.Print((Variant)polygon); // Prints [(50, 50), (150, 50), (150, 150), (50, 150)] [/csharp] [/codeblocks]

members.name: OffsetPolyline
members.signature: public PackedVector2Array[] OffsetPolyline(PackedVector2Array polyline, float delta, int joinType, int endType)
members.summary: Inflates or deflates [param polyline] by [param delta] units (pixels), producing polygons. If [param delta] is positive, makes the polyline grow outward. Returns an array of polygons because inflating/deflating may result in multiple discrete polygons. If [param delta] is negative, returns an empty array. Each polygon's vertices will be rounded as determined by [param join_type]. Each polygon's endpoints will be rounded as determined by [param end_type]. The operation may result in an outer polygon (boundary) and inner polygon (hole) produced which could be distinguished by calling [method is_polygon_clockwise].

members.name: PointIsInsideTriangle
members.signature: public bool PointIsInsideTriangle(Vector2 point, Vector2 a, Vector2 b, Vector2 c)
members.summary: Returns if [param point] is inside the triangle specified by [param a], [param b] and [param c].

members.name: SegmentIntersectsCircle
members.signature: public float SegmentIntersectsCircle(Vector2 segmentFrom, Vector2 segmentTo, Vector2 circlePosition, float circleRadius)
members.summary: Given the 2D segment ([param segment_from], [param segment_to]), returns the position on the segment (as a number between 0 and 1) at which the segment hits the circle that is located at position [param circle_position] and has radius [param circle_radius]. If the segment does not intersect the circle, -1 is returned (this is also the case if the line extending the segment would intersect the circle, but the segment does not).

members.name: SegmentIntersectsSegment
members.signature: public Variant SegmentIntersectsSegment(Vector2 fromA, Vector2 toA, Vector2 fromB, Vector2 toB)
members.summary: Checks if two line segments intersect, with line [code]a[/code] between [param from_a] and [param to_a] and line [code]b[/code] between [param from_b] and [param to_b]. If the line segments intersect, the point of intersection is returned as a [Vector2]. If no intersection takes place, [code]null[/code] is returned.

members.name: TriangulateDelaunay
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array TriangulateDelaunay(PackedVector2Array points)
members.summary: Triangulates the area specified by discrete set of [param points] such that no point is inside the circumcircle of any resulting triangle. Returns a [PackedInt32Array] where each triangle consists of three consecutive point indices into [param points] (i.e. the returned array will have [code]n * 3[/code] elements, with [code]n[/code] being the number of found triangles). If the triangulation did not succeed, an empty [PackedInt32Array] is returned.

members.name: TriangulatePolygon
members.signature: public PackedInt32Array TriangulatePolygon(PackedVector2Array polygon)
members.summary: Triangulates the polygon specified by the points in [param polygon]. Returns a [PackedInt32Array] where each triangle consists of three consecutive point indices into [param polygon] (i.e. the returned array will have [code]n * 3[/code] elements, with [code]n[/code] being the number of found triangles). Output triangles will always be counter clockwise, and the contour will be flipped if it's clockwise. If the triangulation did not succeed, an empty [PackedInt32Array] is returned.

values.name: OPERATION_DIFFERENCE
values.summary: Create regions where subject polygons are filled except where clip polygons are filled.

values.name: OPERATION_INTERSECTION
values.summary: Create regions where both subject and clip polygons are filled.

values.name: OPERATION_UNION
values.summary: Create regions where either subject or clip polygons (or both) are filled.

values.name: OPERATION_XOR
values.summary: Create regions where either subject or clip polygons are filled but not where both are filled.

values.name: END_BUTT
values.summary: Endpoints are squared off with no extension.

values.name: END_JOINED
values.summary: Endpoints are joined using the [enum PolyJoinType] value and the path filled as a polyline.

values.name: END_POLYGON
values.summary: Endpoints are joined using the [enum PolyJoinType] value and the path filled as a polygon.

values.name: END_ROUND
values.summary: Endpoints are rounded off and extended by [code]delta[/code] units.

values.name: END_SQUARE
values.summary: Endpoints are squared off and extended by [code]delta[/code] units.

values.name: JOIN_MITER
values.summary: There's a necessary limit to mitered joins since offsetting edges that join at very acute angles will produce excessively long and narrow "spikes". For any given edge join, when miter offsetting would exceed that maximum distance, "square" joining is applied.

values.name: JOIN_ROUND
values.summary: While flattened paths can never perfectly trace an arc, they are approximated by a series of arc chords.

values.name: JOIN_SQUARE
values.summary: Squaring is applied uniformally at all convex edge joins at [code]1 * delta[/code].

constants.name: OPERATION_UNION
constants.summary: Create regions where either subject or clip polygons (or both) are filled.

constants.name: OPERATION_DIFFERENCE
constants.summary: Create regions where subject polygons are filled except where clip polygons are filled.

constants.name: OPERATION_INTERSECTION
constants.summary: Create regions where both subject and clip polygons are filled.

constants.name: OPERATION_XOR
constants.summary: Create regions where either subject or clip polygons are filled but not where both are filled.

constants.name: JOIN_SQUARE
constants.summary: Squaring is applied uniformally at all convex edge joins at [code]1 * delta[/code].

constants.name: JOIN_ROUND
constants.summary: While flattened paths can never perfectly trace an arc, they are approximated by a series of arc chords.

constants.name: JOIN_MITER
constants.summary: There's a necessary limit to mitered joins since offsetting edges that join at very acute angles will produce excessively long and narrow "spikes". For any given edge join, when miter offsetting would exceed that maximum distance, "square" joining is applied.

constants.name: END_POLYGON
constants.summary: Endpoints are joined using the [enum PolyJoinType] value and the path filled as a polygon.

constants.name: END_JOINED
constants.summary: Endpoints are joined using the [enum PolyJoinType] value and the path filled as a polyline.

constants.name: END_BUTT
constants.summary: Endpoints are squared off with no extension.

constants.name: END_SQUARE
constants.summary: Endpoints are squared off and extended by [code]delta[/code] units.

constants.name: END_ROUND
constants.summary: Endpoints are rounded off and extended by [code]delta[/code] units.
